title: rivea zeylanica var. populifolia
intro: Rivea zeylanica var. populifolia, commonly known as the Indian moonseed or moonseed, is a perennial vine native to tropical Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Rivea zeylanica var. populifolia include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a laxative and to reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
